active_ingredients: Minoxidil 5% topical solution or foam (e.g., Rogaine / Regaine); a 2% solution is also sold, often marketed for women
indications_and_usage: Uses: to regrow hair on the scalp (top of the head) in men and women with hereditary (androgenetic) hair loss - also called male-pattern baldness or female-pattern thinning hair. It is the main FDA-approved over-the-counter treatment for going bald due to genetics. It works best for gradual thinning at the crown/vertex and at the hairline in women; it is not effective for sudden or patchy hair loss, hair loss after childbirth, hair loss from medications, or if there is no family history of hair loss. It does not 'cure' baldness - it slows the loss and partially regrows hair only while you keep using it.
contraindications: Do not use if you are not yet 18 years old; if your hair loss is sudden and/or patchy; if you have no family history of hair loss; if your scalp is red, inflamed, infected, irritated, or painful; or if you use other medicines on the scalp.
warnings_and_cautions: For external use on the scalp only. The effect is not permanent - if you stop applying it, the hair you regrew or kept will gradually be lost again over 3-4 months. It usually takes at least 4 months of twice-daily use (or once-daily for the 5% foam in women) to see results, and results vary; if you do not see regrowth in about 4-6 months, stop. A temporary increase in hair shedding may occur in the first 2-6 weeks (the resting hairs being pushed out before new growth) - this is expected and usually settles. Stop use and ask a doctor if chest pain, rapid heartbeat, faintness, dizziness, sudden unexplained weight gain, swelling of hands or feet, or scalp irritation occurs, or if you apply it to areas other than the scalp (unwanted facial/body hair growth can occur if it spreads). Wash hands after applying; keep away from the eyes; let it dry before lying down.
adverse_reactions: Scalp itching, dryness, flaking, redness or irritation; unwanted hair growth on the face or other areas if the product migrates; rarely lightheadedness, fast heartbeat, fluid retention, or chest pain (from systemic absorption - more likely if overused or used on broken skin).
drug_interactions: Topical use rarely causes systemic interactions; use caution if you take oral antihypertensives (including oral minoxidil) because of potential additive blood-pressure lowering. Do not apply other topical scalp medicines at the same time.
dosage_and_administration: Men: apply 1 mL of the 5% solution, or half a capful of the 5% foam, to the dry scalp in the hair-loss area twice a day. Women: apply the 5% foam once a day, or the 2% solution 1 mL twice a day. Do not apply to more than the scalp, do not use more than directed (more does not work faster and increases side effects), and do not wash the hair for at least 4 hours afterward. Continued use is needed to keep results. See a doctor if there is no improvement after 4-6 months, or earlier if side effects occur. Not for use by anyone under 18.
overdosage: Swallowing the solution, or extensive over-application, can cause systemic minoxidil effects - lightheadedness, fast heartbeat, low blood pressure, fluid retention, and pericardial effects; potentially serious in children. Seek medical help; contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) right away.
